data.text: the tendency to perceive a specific, often meaningful image in a random or ambiguous visual pattern
examples: The scientific explanation for some people is pareidolia, or the human ability to see shapes or make pictures out of randomness. Think of the Rorschach inkblot test.

enrichment.etymology: borrowed from German Pareidolie, from Greek par-  + eídōlon "image, reflection" + German -ie
